valuations.dcf_variant_note: Earnings-Based DCF (also has FCF-Based DCF at $219.68)
valuations.multiples_method: GF Value (proprietary blend of historical multiples, growth-adjusted metrics, past return patterns)
valuations.composite_method: Average of Earnings DCF ($479.28) + GF Value ($535.42) + Graham ($107.97)
valuations.notes: Only source providing all three valuation types. Two-stage 10+10yr model. 20.5% historical EPS growth rate, 4% terminal growth. FCF-based DCF ($219.68) much lower due to heavy capex cycle. Graham Number = sqrt(22.5 x EPS x BVPS). Rated 'Significantly Undervalued'.

valuations.dcf_variant_note: 5-Year Growth Exit DCF (also: 10Y Growth Exit $461.58, 5Y EBITDA Exit $579.33, 10Y EBITDA Exit $643.64)
valuations.multiples_method: Average of P/E Multiples ($549.13) and EV/EBITDA Multiples ($513.05)
valuations.composite_method: Calculated: Average of 5Y Growth DCF ($380.43) + P/E ($549.13) + EV/EBITDA ($513.05)
valuations.notes: Most comprehensive model suite. WACC 8.81%, Cost of Equity 8.99%, Cost of Debt 4.25%, Beta 0.96. Also provides Peter Lynch fair value ($301.64), Earnings Power Value ($105.02), and DDM estimates ($335-$382).

valuations.multiples_method: Relative valuation vs peers (Oracle, ServiceNow, Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, Fortinet) using P/E, EV/EBITDA, EV/EBIT, P/S
valuations.composite_method: Calculated: Average of DCF ($393.82) + Multiples ($386.16) + platform combined value ($393.04) / simplified to (393.82+386.16)/2
valuations.notes: WACC 7.9%, Cost of Equity 8.01%, After-Tax Cost of Debt 4.31%. PV of 5-year cash flows $768.7B, Terminal Value $2.1T, Total Enterprise PV $2.9T. Conclusion: MSFT ~7-9% undervalued.

valuations.dcf_variant_note: Two-stage Free Cash Flow to Equity (FCFE) model
valuations.multiples_method: Industry P/E multiple (26.9x) applied to trailing EPS
valuations.notes: Cost of Equity 6.2% (levered beta 1.016). Projects FCF growing from ~$75B (2026) to ~$165B (2030) to ~$370B (2035). Proprietary 'Fair P/E Ratio' of 44.5x. Community narratives range from $336 (bear) to $627 (bull).

valuations.dcf_variant_note: Blended DCF (Growth Exit, EBITDA Exit, Revenue Multiple terminal variants)
valuations.multiples_method: Overall Fair Value incorporating comparable company multiples models
valuations.notes: Perpetuity growth rates of 2-5%. WACC as discount rate. DCF described as 'significantly outside the interquartile range' for IT sector. Blended fair value pulls slightly below pure DCF due to peer multiples.

valuations.multiples_method: Implied Forward P/E ~39x and FY2026 EV/Sales ~14x (embedded in DCF fair value)
valuations.composite_method: Single fair value estimate (DCF-driven with multiples cross-check)
valuations.notes: Highest credible institutional estimate. Wide Economic Moat rating (switching costs, network effects, cost advantages). Medium Uncertainty. 5Y revenue CAGR ~13%. Raised from $505 after FY2025 Q4 earnings (Jul 2025). 4-star rating (moderately undervalued). Balance sheet: $95B cash vs $43B debt.

valuations.multiples_method: P/E of 25.75x applied to TTM EPS of $15.98
valuations.notes: Most bearish automated DCF. Found MSFT 4.73% overvalued at $365.97. Industry P/E comparison: 25.75x MSFT vs 20.85x sector. Zen Rating: C (Hold). Graham Number behind premium paywall.

valuations.dcf_variant_note: Hybrid 'Real Value' (DCF + multiples + technicals + bankruptcy probability)
valuations.multiples_method: Incorporated into Real Value hybrid model (P/E, P/B, and comparable metrics blended)
valuations.composite_method: Calculated: Average of Real Value ($558.00) + 12-Month Target ($625.41)
valuations.notes: Proprietary hybrid model blending fundamentals, technicals, and bankruptcy probability. Profit margin 36%, ROE 32%, PEG 2.26. 12-month target $625.41. Among the most bullish sources.

valuations.multiples_method: Derived EV/FCF implied valuation from their projected cash flows
valuations.notes: Most bearish credible estimate. WACC 8%, Terminal Growth 2.5%. FCF projections: $75B (2025) to $105B (2029). PV of Terminal Value $1,331.67B. Total EV $1,690.71B. At May 2025 price of $452 implied -49.5% margin of safety. Dec 2024 version yielded $356.20.

valuations.dcf_variant_note: Operating Cash Flow based DCF (not FCF)
valuations.multiples_method: Implied forward P/E ~35x embedded in OCF model terminal multiple of 24.9x
valuations.notes: Uses operating cash flow ($160.5B TTM) instead of FCF, avoiding capex penalty. Growth decays from 15% to 6.5%. Discount rate 10%. Terminal OCF multiple 24.9x. Projects OCF growing to $298B over 6 years. Implies 44% upside.

valuations.dcf_variant_note: Average of three contributor DCFs: $545 (options+moat), $399.24 (12% FCF CAGR), $390 (PEG-based)
valuations.multiples_method: Forward PEG analysis (PEG 2.49 on FY2026 estimates)
valuations.composite_method: Calculated: Average of three DCFs ($545 + $399.24 + $390) + Multiples ($370) / 4
valuations.notes: Community of independent analysts. Bullish ($545) cites economic moat covering ~90% of revenue. Moderate ($399) assumes 12% FCF CAGR. PEG-based ($390) uses forward FY2026 PEG of 2.49. Cluster: $370-$545, generally below Wall Street consensus.

valuations.multiples_method: Analyst consensus growth rate of 10.4% applied to EPS decay framework
valuations.composite_method: Single model output (DCF and multiples integrated in growth decay approach)
valuations.notes: EPS growth decay model starting from current rates and gradually reducing toward sustainable long-term level. Analyst growth input 10.4%. Implies ~17% upside from ~$357. Middle-ground estimate.
